title: fix: retire Polar runtime integration
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Removes the final live Polar dependency: KPI order polling, Worker config, encrypted runtime token, and stale UI naming.
- Makes KPI revenue Stripe/Tinybird-only and removes obsolete Polar reporting scripts and finance forecasting.
- Keeps a concise read-only Polar history/query playbook and preserves the historical D1/Tinybird archives.
- Documents the root cause: pre-migration free daily subscriptions remain in Polar and can generate lifecycle events until they are revoked there.

Checks:
- `npm run build` (`apps/operation/kpi`)
- `npm test` (`apps/operation/finance`, 52 passed)
- Biome and `git diff --check`
- `wrangler deploy --dry-run`

gist.headline: Spring Cleaning: Polar Edition
gist.blurb: We've fully retired our Polar runtime integration, moving our KPI revenue tracking exclusively to Stripe and Tinybird. Just doing a little spring cleaning in the hive's financial backend!
gist.summary: Removed the final live Polar dependencies from the KPI worker and finance scripts, transitioning revenue tracking entirely to Stripe and Tinybird.
gist.impact: Internal operations and revenue tracking are now streamlined through Stripe and Tinybird, with historical Polar data preserved for reference.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot and the Nomnom creature doing some spring cleaning in the server room. Nomnom is eating old Polar reporting scripts while the bee organizes the new Stripe/Tinybird data.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny janitor's hat, sweeping up old paper documents while the round tan nomnom creature happily eats a stack of deprecated reporting scripts. A retro terminal in the background glows with a lime green (#ecf874) dashboard. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
